quote.text: "To build an ERP that speaks their language — both literally and functionally."
quote.desc: We created the global standard software that actually works for the Middle East and Africa because everyone else assumed what works in California would work in Cairo. It doesn't.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.desc: We're building the ERP that construction companies in the Arab world have been waiting for. A system that understands progressive billing, VAT retention, withholding tax, and most importantly — speaks Arabic from day one, not as an afterthought.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.desc: To make professional construction management accessible to every contractor — from Cairo to Riyadh to Dubai. We believe powerful software shouldn't require a Silicon Valley budget or a team of consultants to implement.

chaos.title: The Chaos of Disconnected Tools
chaos.subtitle: We saw construction firms struggling with fragmented systems.

scattered.title: Scattered Tools
scattered.desc: Projects in one app, invoices in another, accounting in Excel — data silos everywhere with duplicate entry and errors.

compliance.title: MENA Compliance Gaps
compliance.desc: Western tools don't support VAT, withholding tax, retention percentages, or admin fees properly.

budget.title: Budget Overruns
budget.desc: No real-time link between project budgets and actual expenses means projects lose money before anyone notices.

procurement.title: Fragmented Procurement
procurement.desc: POs, expenses, and vendor invoices aren't linked, leading to lost receipts and unreconciled payables.

privacyPage.title: TrakCost Privacy Policy
privacyPage.introduction: TrakCost (“TrakCost”, “we”, “our”, or “us”) provides a cloud-based construction ERP platform that unifies project management, financial accounting, procurement, and client/vendor management for construction businesses. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and protect personal information when you visit our website or use our services.

introduction.title: 1. Introduction
introduction.content: TrakCost provides a cloud-based construction ERP platform that unifies project management, financial accounting, procurement, and client/vendor management for construction businesses. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and protect personal information when you visit our website or use our services.

dataCollection.title: 2. Data We Collect
dataCollection.content:
We may collect the following categories of data:

- Account information: name, email address, password (hashed), company name, company size, role, and contact details you provide during sign-up or onboarding.
- Company and project data: project names and types, work items/BOQ, BOQ categories (Excavation, Concrete, Finishings, etc.), task details, budgets, financial records, invoices, purchase orders, expenses, and related documentation.
- Client and vendor data: business names, contact information, linked projects, invoices, POs, and expense history.
- Financial and transactional data: invoice headers and line items, tax details (VAT, withholding tax, retention percentages, admin fees, cost-plus fees), payment records, journal entries, reconciliation data, and financial reports.
- Usage data: pages visited, feature usage, configuration settings, and time spent within modules such as Projects, Accounting, Invoicing, Purchase Orders, Expenses, and CRM.
- Technical data: IP address, browser type, device information, and basic log data collected to secure authentication and sessions through our modern infra (Supabase & Next.js).

You are responsible for ensuring that any personal data of third parties (such as your clients, vendors, or employees) entered into TrakCost has been collected and shared in accordance with applicable laws.

dataUsage.title: 3. How We Use Data
dataUsage.content:
We use collected data to:

- Provide and operate the TrakCost ERP platform, including project management, invoicing, accounting, procurement, and reporting modules.
- Configure your environment (company settings, chart of accounts, roles and permissions, localization settings, and lookup values).
- Maintain security of the platform, including authentication, authorization, and audit logging.
- Improve product performance and user experience, including analyzing anonymized or aggregated usage patterns.
- Provide onboarding, training, and customer support.
- Communicate with you about service updates, feature changes, and marketing content where permitted by law.
- Comply with legal obligations, including tax, financial, and audit requirements in the jurisdictions where we operate.

legalBases.title: 4. Legal Bases for Processing
legalBases.content:
Depending on your location, we process personal data on the following legal bases:

- Performance of a contract: to deliver the services under our agreement with you or your company.
- Legitimate interests: to secure, maintain, and improve TrakCost and to communicate important service-related information.
- Consent: for specific marketing or optional product-related communications, where required.
- Legal obligations: to meet applicable financial, tax, accounting, and regulatory requirements, particularly for MENA-region compliance.

dataSharing.title: 5. Data Sharing and Transfers
dataSharing.content:
We may share data with:

- Service providers: infrastructure, database, authentication, email delivery, analytics, and support tools that help us operate TrakCost. These providers are bound by confidentiality and data protection obligations.
- Professional advisors: legal, accounting, or security consultants under appropriate confidentiality terms.
- Authorities: when required to comply with applicable law, legal process, or enforceable governmental requests.

We do not sell your personal data. If we engage in any corporate transaction (such as merger, acquisition, or asset sale), your data may be transferred as part of that transaction subject to continued protection.

internationalTransfers.title: 6. International Data Transfers
internationalTransfers.content: TrakCost is built for the MENA region with support for local tax and Arabic/RTL language, but our infrastructure may be hosted in other regions. Where data is transferred across borders, we implement appropriate safeguards in line with applicable law.

dataRetention.title: 7. Data Retention
dataRetention.content:
We retain personal data for as long as:

- Your account or subscription remains active.
- Necessary to provide services, support, and reporting.
- Required by applicable law. For financial, tax, and accounting records, we typically retain data for 10 years as per statutory requirements in many MENA jurisdictions (including Egypt and the Gulf).

Upon termination of service, we may anonymize or aggregate data for analytics, and we will delete or securely archive identifiable data within a commercially reasonable timeframe, subject to legal retention obligations.

yourRights.title: 8. Your Rights
yourRights.content:
Subject to local law, you may have the right to:

- Access personal data we hold about you.
- Correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
- Request deletion of your personal data, subject to our legal obligations.
- Object to or restrict certain processing activities.
- Withdraw consent where processing is based on consent.

Requests can be submitted via our contact channels listed on the website (e.g., Contact or Support pages).

cookies.title: 9. Cookies and Similar Technologies
cookies.content:
Our website and app may use cookies or similar technologies to:

- Maintain sessions and authentication.
- Remember language and interface preferences (including Arabic/English selection and RTL/LTR layout).
- Analyze usage to improve performance.

Where required, we will present a consent banner allowing you to manage cookie preferences.

childrenPrivacy.title: 10. Children’s Privacy
childrenPrivacy.content: TrakCost is a business-focused ERP platform and is not directed to individuals under 18. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If you believe a child has provided data, contact us so we can remove it.

policyChanges.title: 11. Changes to This Policy
policyChanges.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will indicate the “Last updated” date and, where appropriate, notify you through the platform or by email.

contact.title: 12. Contact
contact.content: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or how your data is handled, please contact us using the details on the Contact or Support page of the TrakCost website.

termsPage.title: TrakCost Terms of Service
termsPage.introduction: These Terms of Service (“Terms”) govern your access to and use of the TrakCost website, applications, and services (collectively, the “Service”). By creating an account, starting a free trial, or using the Service in any way, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you are using the Service on behalf of a company or other legal entity, you represent that you have authority to bind that entity, and “you” refers to that entity.

agreement.title: 1. Agreement
agreement.content:
These Terms of Service (“Terms”) govern your access to and use of the TrakCost website, applications, and services (collectively, the “Service”). By creating an account, starting a free trial, or using the Service in any way, you agree to be bound by these Terms.

If you are using the Service on behalf of a company or other legal entity, you represent that you have authority to bind that entity, and “you” refers to that entity.

service.title: 2. Service Description
service.content:
TrakCost is a cloud-based, multi-tenant construction ERP platform designed for contractors and construction firms, with modules for project management, invoicing, purchase orders, expense management, double-entry accounting, CRM, and related features. The platform is optimized for the MENA region with built-in VAT, withholding, retention, and Arabic/RTL support.

We may add, modify, or remove features over time, including releasing new modules such as mobile apps, equipment management, advanced analytics, or integrations as indicated in our roadmap.

registration.title: 3. Account Registration and Access
registration.content:
- To use TrakCost, you must create an account and provide accurate and complete information.
- You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your login credentials and for all activities under your account.
- You must promptly notify us if you suspect any unauthorized access or use of your account.
- We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these Terms or pose a security risk to the Service or other users.

payments.title: 4. Subscription Plans and Payments
payments.content:
TrakCost offers multiple tiers, including Free, Pro, and Enterprise, each with defined features, usage limits, and pricing as displayed on the Pricing page. Specific terms for billing cycles, renewal, and cancellation will be shown at checkout and may be updated periodically.

- Free tier: limited usage (for example, user and project caps, basic features) for solo contractors and small teams.
- Pro tier: expanded features such as full accounting, POs, and reporting for growing construction companies.
- Enterprise tier: advanced features such as audit trails, API access, and dedicated support for larger firms.

Fees are typically billed in advance on a monthly or annual basis. Unless otherwise specified, subscriptions automatically renew at the then-current rates unless cancelled before the end of the current term.

acceptableUse.title: 5. Acceptable Use
acceptableUse.content:
You agree not to:

- Use the Service in violation of any applicable laws, including tax, accounting, or data protection laws in your jurisdiction.
- Upload or store unlawful, offensive, or infringing content.
- Attempt to gain unauthorized access to other tenants’ data, the underlying infrastructure, or restricted areas of the Service.
- Interfere with or disrupt the integrity, performance, or security of the Service.
- Reverse engineer, decompile, or otherwise attempt to derive source code from the Service, except to the extent such restrictions are prohibited by law.

We may investigate and take appropriate action (including account suspension or termination) if we suspect misuse.

ownership.title: 6. Customer Data and Ownership
ownership.content:
You retain all rights to the data you and your users upload or generate within TrakCost (“Customer Data”), including projects, work items, financial records, documents, and client/vendor records. We do not claim ownership of Customer Data.

By using the Service, you grant us a limited, non-exclusive license to process Customer Data solely for the purpose of providing and improving the Service, including security, support, and analytics in aggregated or anonymized form.

You are solely responsible for:

- The accuracy, quality, and legality of Customer Data.
- Ensuring you have all necessary rights and consents to store and process Customer Data within TrakCost.

dataSecurity.title: 7. Data Protection and Security
dataSecurity.content:
We implement technical and organizational measures designed to protect Customer Data, including:

- Multi-tenant architecture with strict isolation of company data.
- Role-based access control, allowing you to assign permissions by role.
- Authentication and session management with secure identity services and row-level security on database tables.
- Audit logs capturing key user actions and changes to support compliance.

Your use of the Service is also governed by our Privacy Policy, which describes how we handle personal data.

availability.title: 8. Service Availability and Support
availability.content:
We aim to keep TrakCost available and performant; however, the Service may occasionally be unavailable due to maintenance, upgrades, or circumstances beyond our control.

- We may provide onboarding guidance, help center resources, and direct support depending on your subscription tier.
- For Enterprise customers, additional support and service levels may be specified in a separate agreement.

thirdParty.title: 9. Third-Party Services
thirdParty.content: TrakCost may rely on or integrate with third-party services (for example, authentication, email, analytics, or future integrations). Use of those services may be subject to separate terms and policies. We are not responsible for third-party services outside our reasonable control.

intellectualProperty.title: 10. Intellectual Property
intellectualProperty.content:
All rights, title, and interest in and to the Service (including software, user interface design, trademarks, logos, and documentation) are owned by TrakCost or its licensors. These Terms do not grant you any ownership interest in the Service or its content.

You may not use our trademarks, branding, or visual identity without prior written permission.

beta.title: 11. Beta and Roadmap Features
beta.content: From time to time, we may offer early access to beta or “coming soon” features such as mobile apps, equipment management, or advanced analytics. These features may be offered “as is,” with reduced or different support, and may change or be discontinued at any time.

portability.title: 12. Data Portability
portability.content: Upon written request or via available platform export tools, you have the right to export your Customer Data (including audit logs, project financials, and ledgers) in a structured, commonly used format for archival or transition purposes.

disclaimer.title: 13. Disclaimers
disclaimer.content:
The Service is provided “as is” and “as available.” To the fullest extent permitted by law, we disclaim all warranties, express or implied, including fitness for a particular purpose, non-infringement, or that the Service will be error-free or uninterrupted.

You are responsible for:

- How you configure and use TrakCost (chart of accounts, tax settings, project data).
- Compliance of your use of the Service with applicable legal, tax, and accounting requirements.
- TrakCost is not a professional firm: The Service is a software tool designed to support construction management and accounting. TrakCost does not provide legal, tax, or professional accounting advice. You should consult with qualified professionals for specific advice related to your business.

liability.title: 14. Limitation of Liability
liability.content:
To the maximum extent permitted by law:

- TrakCost will not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or for any loss of profits, revenues, data, or goodwill.
- Our total aggregate liability arising out of or relating to the Service or these Terms will not exceed the amount you paid to use the Service in the 12 months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

These limitations apply whether or not we have been advised of the possibility of such damages.

termination.title: 15. Term and Termination
termination.content:
These Terms apply from your first use of the Service and continue until terminated.

We may suspend or terminate your access to the Service if:

- You breach these Terms or fail to pay applicable fees.
- We are required to do so by law.
- We discontinue the Service in whole or in part.

Upon termination, your right to access and use the Service ceases. We will handle Customer Data in accordance with our Privacy Policy and any applicable data export options available to you.

changes.title: 16. Changes to Terms
changes.content: We may revise these Terms from time to time. We will post updates on our website and indicate the “Last updated” date. Continued use of the Service after changes take effect constitutes your acceptance of the revised Terms.

governingLaw.title: 17. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
governingLaw.content: These Terms and any disputes arising out of or relating to them or the Service will be governed by the laws of the jurisdiction specified on our website or order form, without regard to conflict of law provisions. Any disputes shall be resolved in the courts of that jurisdiction, unless otherwise agreed in writing.

contact.title: 18. Contact
contact.content: For questions about these Terms, please contact us using the details on the TrakCost Contact or Support page.

securityPage.title: TrakCost Security
securityPage.subtitle: Enterprise-grade protection for your construction and accounting data.

overview.title: 1. Security Overview
overview.content: TrakCost is designed as a secure, multi-tenant construction ERP platform that isolates each company’s data while enabling collaboration within teams. Security is built into our architecture, development practices, and operational processes.

architecture.title: 2. Application and Data Architecture
architecture.content:
- Multi-tenant design: each company is logically isolated to prevent cross-tenant access.
- Row-Level Security (RLS): enforced at the database level to restrict access to records based on tenant and user context.
- Role-based access control (RBAC): configurable roles and permissions allow you to restrict access by function (for example, project manager, accountant, procurement).
- Module separation: projects, invoices, POs, expenses, and accounting records are all linked through controlled relationships, ensuring consistent data integrity.

authentication.title: 3. Authentication and Access Control
authentication.content:
- Double-entry accounting integrity: all financial transactions are recorded with linked journal entries through our core accounting engine, ensuring that every debit has a corresponding credit and leaving no "orphan" data ghosts.
- Supabase Identity & Auth: secure email/password authentication via Supabase Auth, with protected routes and session management.
- Modern Infrastructure: built on Next.js for secure server-side logic and PostgreSQL for robust multi-tenant isolation.

protection.title: 4. Data Protection in Transit and at Rest
protection.content:
- All connections to the TrakCost application and APIs are protected via HTTPS/TLS to encrypt data in transit.
- Data at rest is stored in managed PostgreSQL databases with industry-standard encryption.
- Robust multi-tenant isolation ensures that company_id filters are enforced at the query level.
- Regular backups are performed to enable recovery in case of data loss or corruption.

audit.title: 5. Audit Trail and Logging
audit.content:
- TrakCost includes audit logging of key system actions, capturing who did what and when, to support accountability and compliance.
- Changes to important records (such as financial documents, settings, and user roles) are tracked for review.
- System and security logs are monitored for suspicious patterns or potential abuse.

compliance.title: 6. Financial and Compliance Controls
compliance.content:
- Double-entry accounting: all financial transactions are recorded with linked journal entries, ensuring accurate financial trails.
- Accounting periods: open/close controls help maintain data integrity for finalized periods.
- MENA tax support: VAT, withholding, retention, and admin fees are consistently calculated and recorded across invoices and POs, supporting local compliance.

operational.title: 7. Operational Security
operational.content:
- Access to production systems is restricted to authorized team members following the principle of least privilege.
- Configuration and secret management follow secure handling practices.
- Changes to the platform are tested and reviewed before deployment to minimize risk.

residency.title: 8. Data Residency and Localization
residency.content:
- TrakCost is built for the MENA region with support for local tax rules, Arabic/RTL UI, and bilingual experiences, which helps organizations align with local requirements.
- Details about specific hosting regions or data centers can be obtained from us upon request, to assist with your compliance reviews.

responsibilities.title: 9. Customer Responsibilities
responsibilities.content:
While TrakCost provides a secure platform, you also play a role in protecting your environment:

- Use strong, unique passwords and avoid sharing credentials.
- Configure roles and permissions carefully, granting only necessary access.
- Review audit logs and financial reports regularly for unusual activity.
- Ensure your own devices, networks, and browsers are kept secure and updated.

reporting.title: 10. Reporting Security Issues
reporting.content: If you discover a potential security vulnerability or suspicious activity, contact us immediately through the channels listed on the TrakCost website. Please provide as much detail as possible so we can investigate and address the issue promptly.
